content: Photo Organizer follows a layered architecture: User Interface Layer: CLI and GUI interfaces; Application Layer: Core application logic and state management; Service Layer: Business logic for image analysis, categorization, and file operations; Model Layer: Data structures for images, categories, and reports; Utility Layer: Helper functions and utilities.

The directory structure includes: docs/ for documentation (examples.md, installation.md, quick_start.md, user_guide.md, and technical/); pyinstaller/ for PyInstaller configuration; src/ for source code (photo_organizer/ with models/, services/ including vision/, ui/, and utils/); and tests/ (integration/ and unit/).
Models include `Image` for image files with metadata and features, `Category` for organizing images, and `CategoryTree` for a hierarchy of categories.

Services include `ImageAnalysisService` for analyzing images, `CategorizationService` for categorizing images, `FileOperationsService` for file operations, and `ReportingService` for generating reports.
User Interfaces include `CLIParser` for command-line arguments, `CLIProgressReporter` for reporting progress to the console, and `GUIApp` as the main GUI application.
Core Components include `ApplicationCore` for main application logic, `StateManager` for managing application state, and `TaskScheduler` for parallel processing.
Each module should have a single responsibility.
Related functionality should be grouped in the same package.
Implementation files should be accompanied by test files.
Import order should be: 1. Standard library imports, 2. Third-party library imports, 3. Application imports.

Class structure should have: Public methods first, Protected methods (prefixed with `_`) next, Private methods (prefixed with `__`) last, Class methods and static methods grouped with related instance methods.
Unit tests mirror the structure of the source code.
Integration tests focus on component interactions.

The application uses a state machine with the following states: `IDLE`: Ready to process images; `RUNNING`: Processing images; `PAUSED`: Processing paused; `CANCELING`: Canceling operation; `COMPLETED`: Processing completed; `FAILED`: Processing failed.
